agent_output: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"A request to integrate two previously independent bounded contexts — Warehouse Management and Sales/Order Management — so that order placement becomes coupled to real-time stock availability. This is a classic cross-context integration challenge where the boundary between inventory truth and sales workflow must be carefully negotiated.","bounded_context":"Cross-cutting concern spanning Warehouse Management (Context A) and Sales/Order Management (Context B). The feature request implies creating a new integration seam, not merging the contexts.","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"StockLevel","definition":"The current integer quantity of a Product physically available in the warehouse. Owned exclusively by Context A (Warehouse Management). Must be >= 0."},{"term":"Order","definition":"A customer's intent to purchase one or more products, composed of OrderLines. Owned by Context B (Sales/Order Management)."},{"term":"OrderLine","definition":"A single line item on an Order, referencing a Product by ID and specifying a Quantity requested."},{"term":"Stock Reservation","definition":"A soft hold on a quantity of stock claimed by an Order but not yet physically picked. This is a NEW concept required by the integration — it does not exist in either context today."},{"term":"Stock Available Indicator","definition":"A read-model projection showing whether a Product currently has sufficient stock. This is an eventually consistent view, NOT a transactional guarantee."},{"term":"Order Blocking","definition":"The act of rejecting or holding an Order because the requested quantity exceeds available (unreserved) stock at the time of placement."},{"term":"Available Stock","definition":"StockLevel minus the sum of all active Stock Reservations. This is the quantity actually orderable. Distinct from StockLevel, which is the physical count."}]},"business_rules":[{"id":"BR-1","rule":"StockLevel for any Product must never go below zero.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"A negative stock level represents a physical impossibility — you cannot pick items that do not exist. If violated, the warehouse data is corrupt.","testable_assertion":"For all Products at all times: StockLevel >= 0"},{"id":"BR-2","rule":"Context A (Warehouse Management) is the sole system of record for StockLevel. No other context may directly mutate StockLevel.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"If multiple contexts can write StockLevel, data integrity is destroyed. This is an ownership invariant for the aggregate.","testable_assertion":"Only Warehouse Management commands (PickItems, ReceiveItems) can change StockLevel. No direct writes from Context B."},{"id":"BR-3","rule":"An Order should be blocked if, at the time of placement, Available Stock (StockLevel minus active Reservations) is insufficient to fulfill all OrderLines.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"This is a business policy. A VP could decide to allow overselling (backorders), partial fulfillment, or waitlisting. The blocking threshold is a policy decision, not a physical constraint.","testable_assertion":"Given Product X has Available Stock of 5, when an Order is placed with OrderLine for Product X quantity 6, then the Order is blocked."},{"id":"BR-4","rule":"The 'stock available' indicator shown to the sales team is eventually consistent — it may lag behind the true StockLevel by a bounded time window.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Real-time is aspirational. The indicator is a read-model projection. Business can tune the acceptable staleness window.","testable_assertion":"The stock available indicator reflects StockLevel changes within the agreed staleness SLA (e.g., < 5 seconds)."},{"id":"BR-5","rule":"Stock Reservations must have a bounded lifetime. A reservation that is not converted to a pick within a configurable time window must expire and release the reserved quantity.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Without expiry, abandoned carts or failed orders permanently reduce Available Stock. The timeout value is a tunable policy.","testable_assertion":"A Stock Reservation not confirmed within T minutes is automatically released, and Available Stock increases accordingly."},{"id":"BR-6","rule":"The sum of all active Stock Reservations for a Product must never exceed that Product's StockLevel.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Reserving more stock than physically exists would allow multiple orders to claim the same items, leading to fulfillment failures and corrupt state.","testable_assertion":"For all Products: SUM(active reservations) <= StockLevel"}],"acceptance_criteria":[{"id":"AC-1","given":"Product X has StockLevel 10 and no active reservations","when":"A customer places an Order with an OrderLine for Product X, quantity 5","then":"The Order is accepted AND a Stock Reservation of 5 is created for Product X, reducing Available Stock to 5"},{"id":"AC-2","given":"Product X has StockLevel 10 and existing reservations totaling 8","when":"A customer places an Order with an OrderLine for Product X, quantity 3","then":"The Order is blocked because Available Stock (2) is less than requested quantity (3)"},{"id":"AC-3","given":"Product X has StockLevel 10 and existing reservations totaling 8","when":"A customer places an Order with an OrderLine for Product X, quantity 2","then":"The Order is accepted AND a reservation of 2 is created, reducing Available Stock to 0"},{"id":"AC-4","given":"A Stock Reservation for Product X, quantity 5, was created 30 minutes ago (exceeding the expiry window)","when":"The reservation expiry process runs","then":"The reservation is released AND Available Stock for Product X increases by 5"},{"id":"AC-5","given":"Product X has StockLevel 10 and no reservations","when":"The sales team views the stock available indicator for Product X","then":"The indicator shows 'available' with quantity 10 (within the staleness SLA)"},{"id":"AC-6","given":"An Order contains OrderLines for Product X (qty 3) and Product Y (qty 2)","when":"Product X has Available Stock 3 but Product Y has Available Stock 1","then":"The entire Order is blocked — partial acceptance is not permitted unless business policy explicitly allows it"},{"id":"AC-7","given":"Two customers simultaneously place Orders each requesting the last 1 unit of Product X","when":"Both orders are processed","then":"Exactly one Order is accepted and one is blocked. The StockLevel never goes below 0 and reservations never exceed StockLevel."}],"edge_cases":[{"scenario":"Two concurrent orders compete for the last unit of stock","expected_behavior":"Only one order succeeds. The integration must handle concurrency at the reservation level — optimistic locking, serialized command processing, or equivalent. No double-booking."},{"scenario":"Stock is received (StockLevel increases) while an order is being blocked","expected_behavior":"The order was evaluated against a point-in-time snapshot. It remains blocked. The customer may retry. We do NOT retroactively unblock."},{"scenario":"An Order contains multiple OrderLines, some with sufficient stock and some without","expected_behavior":"The entire Order is blocked. All-or-nothing at the Order level unless the business explicitly defines a partial fulfillment policy."},{"scenario":"The Warehouse Management system is temporarily unavailable","expected_behavior":"Orders cannot be validated against stock. Business must decide: fail-open (accept orders optimistically) or fail-closed (reject all orders until stock data is available). This is a critical policy decision."},{"scenario":"StockLevel is updated to 0 via a stock correction while active reservations exist","expected_behavior":"BR-6 is violated if reservations exceed the corrected StockLevel. The system must reconcile — either cancel affected reservations and notify, or prevent the correction until reservations are cleared."},{"scenario":"A Product referenced in an OrderLine does not exist in Context A","expected_behavior":"The Order should be blocked or rejected. A Product that the warehouse doesn't know about cannot have stock checked."},{"scenario":"Stock available indicator shows 'available' but by the time the customer submits, stock is reserved by another order","expected_behavior":"The indicator was eventually consistent and correct at read-time. The order is blocked at submission time. The UI should handle this gracefully with a clear message."}],"business_impact":{"financial":"Blocking orders on insufficient stock prevents overselling, which avoids costly fulfillment failures, customer refunds, and expedited shipping for backorders. However, overly aggressive blocking (e.g., stale data, reservation leaks) could reject valid orders and directly reduce revenue.","operational":"Introduces a hard runtime dependency between Sales and Warehouse contexts. Warehouse system downtime now directly impacts order placement — a coupling that did not previously exist. Requires a new Stock Reservation lifecycle (create, confirm, expire, release) that must be operationally monitored.","user":"Sales team gains a real-time stock indicator, improving confidence in order promises. Customers may experience order rejections they didn't see before (previously, oversold orders were handled downstream). UX must clearly communicate why an order is blocked and what the customer can do about it.","risk":"HIGH — This integration introduces tight cross-context coupling. The biggest risk is the Stock Reservation concept: if reservations leak (never expire, never release), Available Stock silently degrades to zero even when the warehouse is full. Secondary risk: the 'real-time' indicator creates a false sense of certainty in an eventually consistent system, leading to user frustration when blocking contradicts what the UI showed moments ago.","stakeholders_affected":[{"group":"Customers","impact":"Will now see order rejections at placement time. Better than learning about stockouts after payment, but requires clear UX messaging."},{"group":"Sales Team","impact":"Gains stock visibility (positive), but must learn to interpret eventually consistent data. May receive customer complaints about orders blocked despite indicator showing 'available'."},{"group":"Warehouse Operations","impact":"StockLevel now has external consumers making real-time decisions. Any stock correction, inventory count, or receiving delay has immediate downstream impact on order acceptance."},{"group":"Engineering / Platform","impact":"Must build and maintain the integration layer, Stock Reservation lifecycle, read-model projections, and concurrency controls. Ongoing operational burden."},{"group":"Finance","impact":"Fewer refunds and fulfillment cost overruns from overselling. But potential revenue loss from false-negative blocks must be monitored."}]},"confidence":{"level":52,"reasoning":"The feature request is underspecified in several critical areas: (1) no decision on fail-open vs. fail-closed when warehouse is unavailable, (2) no specification of whether partial fulfillment is allowed, (3) the concept of Stock Reservation is entirely new and its lifecycle is not defined in the request, (4) 'real-time' is ambiguous — what staleness is acceptable? These are all ambiguities that materially affect correctness.","high_confidence_areas":["StockLevel >= 0 is an invariant","Context A owns StockLevel exclusively","A new concept (Stock Reservation or equivalent) is required to bridge the contexts","The stock indicator is necessarily eventually consistent"],"low_confidence_areas":["Fail-open vs fail-closed policy when Warehouse is unavailable","Partial fulfillment policy for multi-line orders","Acceptable staleness window for the stock indicator","Reservation expiry timeout value","Whether blocking means rejection or hold-for-review"],"assumptions":["Stock Reservation is the correct integration pattern (vs. synchronous stock check with no reservation)","All-or-nothing order validation is the default policy for multi-line orders","The business wants hard blocking, not soft warnings","The stock indicator is a read-model projection, not a live query to Context A"]},"escalations":[{"type":"ambiguity","description":"The feature says 'automatically block' but does not define what 'block' means. Is the order rejected outright? Held in a pending state for review? Queued for retry when stock arrives? Each interpretation has very different domain modeling implications.","affected_stakeholders":["Sales Team","Customers","Engineering"],"options":["Reject the order immediately with an error message","Place the order in a 'Blocked' state that can be unblocked when stock arrives","Queue the order as a backorder with an estimated fulfillment date"],"recommendation":"Reject the order immediately for V1. Backorder/hold semantics add significant complexity and should be a separate feature."},{"type":"ambiguity","description":"'Real-time stock available indicator' — what staleness is acceptable? True real-time requires synchronous queries or event streaming to Context A, with significant infrastructure cost. Eventually consistent with a 5-second window is dramatically simpler.","affected_stakeholders":["Sales Team","Engineering"],"options":["Sub-second via synchronous API call to warehouse","< 5 second staleness via event-driven read model","< 1 minute via periodic polling"],"recommendation":"Event-driven read model with < 5 second staleness. True real-time is architecturally expensive and the indicator is advisory, not transactional."},{"type":"ambiguity","description":"No policy defined for warehouse system unavailability. Should order placement fail-open (accept optimistically, risk overselling) or fail-closed (reject all orders, lose revenue)?","affected_stakeholders":["Customers","Sales Team","Finance","Warehouse Operations"],"options":["Fail-closed: reject orders when stock cannot be verified","Fail-open: accept orders optimistically, reconcile later","Degrade gracefully: accept orders but flag them for manual stock verification"],"recommendation":"Fail-closed for V1 with a clear error message. Overselling was presumably the problem this feature solves — fail-open reintroduces it."},{"type":"missing_context","description":"Stock Reservation is a new domain concept that does not exist in either context today. It is unclear which bounded context should own it. If Context A owns it, Sales must make cross-context commands to reserve. If Context B owns it, Sales is making stock promises without warehouse authority.","affected_stakeholders":["Engineering","Warehouse Operations"],"options":["Context A owns reservations (warehouse authority over stock allocation)","A new Inventory Allocation context owns reservations (clean separation)","Context B owns reservations as a projection (simpler but weaker consistency)"],"recommendation":"Context A should own Stock Reservations. The warehouse is the authority on physical stock, and reservations are a constraint on that stock. Alternatively, a dedicated Inventory Allocation context could emerge if the reservation logic becomes complex."}],"rejection_reasons":[{"violation":"Directly querying or writing Context A's StockLevel from Context B would violate bounded context boundaries and create tight coupling.","business_rule_broken":"BR-2"},{"violation":"Any implementation that allows two concurrent orders to both succeed for the last unit of stock violates the StockLevel non-negativity invariant.","business_rule_broken":"BR-1, BR-6"},{"violation":"An implementation with no reservation expiry mechanism will eventually deadlock Available Stock at zero, silently preventing all orders.","business_rule_broken":"BR-5"}]}
agent_output_excerpt: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"A request to integrate two previously independent bounded contexts — Warehouse Management and Sales/Order Management — so that order placement becomes coupled to real-time stock availability. This is a classic cross-context integration challenge where the boundary between inventory truth and sales workflow must be carefully negotiated.","bounded_context":"Cross-cutting concern spanning Warehouse Management (Context A) and Sales/Order Management (Context B)
